zh.lifeSummary: 當Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE於大約1185年出生在比利時福拉安德倫時，他的父親Rasse DE GAVRE DE LIEDKERKE V25歲，母親Claire D’Eksaerde van Herzele26歲。他於1208年與Sophie Von SCHOTEN BREDA結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1242年11月23日享年58歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE于大约1185年出生在比利时福拉安德倫时，他的父亲Rasse DE GAVRE DE LIEDKERKE V25岁，母亲Claire D’Eksaerde van Herzele26岁。他于1208年与Sophie Von SCHOTEN BREDA结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1242年11月23日享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: GRAVE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Alejandro, Domingo, Florentino, Jose, Juan, Octavio, Rafael.

English: occupational name from Middle English grayve, grayve, greve ‘steward, bailiff, manorial officer who managed the lord's demesne farm, headman of a town or village’, a borrowing from Old Norse greifi ‘earl, count’. Compare the synonymous Grieve and Reeve .
English: topographic name from Middle English grave ‘pit’ (Old English græf).

variants.name: Graeve
meanings: German (also Gräve): variant of Grave .

variants.name: Graves
meanings: English (northern and eastern England): variant of Grave , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
English: alternatively, a variant of   Greaves   or   Grieves  .
Altered form of German Greff , with the addition of excrescent -s, a common feature of Americanized surnames.

variants.name: Graven
meanings: Dutch and North German: variant of   Grave  .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads so named, from Old Norse grǫf ‘hollow, depression’.

variants.name: Gravier
meanings: French: topographic name derived from Old French grave ‘gravel’ (see Grave ), or a habitational name from (Le) Gravier, the name of several places in various parts of France.

variants.name: Gravois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Francois, Gaston, Laurent.

French: topographic name for someone who lived in an area of coarse gravel, derived from Old French grave ‘gravel’ (see Grave ), or a habitational name from (Les) Gravois, the name of several places in the northern part of France.

variants.name: Grieve
meanings: English:

occupational name for a steward or estate manager, from Middle English greve ‘steward, farm bailiff, or principal officer of a town’ (Old Northumbrian English grǣfa). Compare Reeve and Sheriff . The name is characteristic of northeastern England and southern and southeastern Scotland.
alternatively, a variant of   Grave  . These words were originally distinct, but some confusion has occurred as a result of the close similarity in both form and meaning.

variants.name: Gravel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Normand, Gaston, Jean Guy, Lucien, Pierre, Serge, Donat, Felicien, Fernand, Gilles, Michel.
English and Welsh (of Norman origin): probably a habitational name from La Gravelle in La Mayenne and Saint-Hilaire-la-Gravelle in Loir-et-Cher, France.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French grave ‘gravel’ (see Grave ).

variants.name: Grayson
meanings: English (northern): variant of Graveson, ‘son of the steward, farm bailiff, or village headman’. From the North Midlands through Yorkshire to the north-west, the source is Middle English grayve, grayve, greve, a borrowing of Old Norse greifi ‘earl, count’ (see Grave ). In Durham and Northumberland the source is Middle English greve, from Old (Northumbrian) English grǣfa with the same meaning (see Grieve ).
English: possibly a habitational name from Greysouthen (Cumbria), recorded as Graysone in 1505.

nameMeanings.name: Rasse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Joris.
German: topographic name from Middle High German rœsse ‘kiln’, in particular one used for drying hemp and flax or for burning lime.
English and Scottish (Lanarkshire): variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Either an adoption of the Gaelic topographic term ros ‘headland’ ( compare Glen , Kyle ) or a transferred use of the Scottish surname, which is borne by a large and ancient family whose members have played a major role in Scottish history. Although still very popular in Scotland, the name is now in widespread general use.

nameMeanings.name: IX
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: probably a contracted form of  Jep , vernacular form of   Jacob   .

nameMeanings.name: "dit
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Dorothy .

variants.name: Dorothy
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

nameMeanings.name: L'Ainé"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE was born about 1185, in Flanders, Belgium, his father, Rasse DE GAVRE DE LIEDKERKE V, was 25 and his mother, Claire D’Eksaerde van Herzele, was 26. He married Sophie Von SCHOTEN BREDA in 1208. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 23 November 1242, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE est né environ 1185, à Flandre, Belgique, son père, Rasse DE GAVRE DE LIEDKERKE V, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Claire D’Eksaerde van Herzele, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Sophie Von SCHOTEN BREDA en 1208. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 23 novembre 1242, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE um 1185 in Vlaanderen, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rasse DE GAVRE DE LIEDKERKE V 25 und seine Mutter Claire D’Eksaerde van Herzele 26. Er hat 1208 Sophie Von SCHOTEN BREDA geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 23. November 1242 im Alter von 58 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE è nato il circa 1185 nel Vlaanderen, Belgio, il suo padre, Rasse DE GAVRE DE LIEDKERKE V, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Claire D’Eksaerde van Herzele, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Sophie Von SCHOTEN BREDA nel 1208. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 23 novembre 1242, all'età di 58 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVEが約1185年にベルギーvラアンデレンで生まれた時，父Rasse DE GAVRE DE LIEDKERKE Vは25歳，母Claire D’Eksaerde van Herzeleは26歳であった。1208年にSophie Von SCHOTEN BREDAと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1242年11月23日に58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE(이)가 대략 1185년 벨기에 블라아더에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rasse DE GAVRE DE LIEDKERKE V(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Claire D’Eksaerde van Herzele(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1208년 Sophie Von SCHOTEN BREDA(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1242년 11월 23일 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE nasceu aproximadamente 1185, em Vlaanderen, Bélgica, seu pai, Rasse DE GAVRE DE LIEDKERKE V, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Claire D’Eksaerde van Herzele, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Sophie Von SCHOTEN BREDA em 1208. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 23 de novembro de 1242, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE родился около 1185 г. в местности влаандирен, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Rasse DE GAVRE DE LIEDKERKE V было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Claire D’Eksaerde van Herzele, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Sophie Von SCHOTEN BREDA в 1208 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 23 ноября 1242 г. в возрасте 58 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE nació aproximadamente 1185, en Vlaanderen, Bélgica, su papá, Rasse DE GAVRE DE LIEDKERKE V, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Claire D’Eksaerde van Herzele, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Sophie Von SCHOTEN BREDA en 1208. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 23 de noviembre de 1242, a la edad de 58 años.
